zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡偏多整理
zh.content:
当前SOL处于低位横盘震荡结构，价格在74.3至75美元之间窄幅整理，整体呈弱势修复后的横盘蓄势形态，多空暂未形成有效突破方向

技术指标方面，价格略高于SMA20(74.28)显示轻微偏多，但MACD虽为正值0.66，动能信号不完整，RSI缺失导致趋势强度判断有限

支撑位关注74.3与74整数关口，下方若失守将回测73.6区域；上方75为短线强阻，突破后有望测试75.5及76压力区间

操作建议以区间交易为主，高抛低吸为核心策略，等待75上方放量突破再顺势做多，跌破74则需谨慎并控制风险仓位

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
continues to trade within a narrow consolidation range after a modest 24-hour gain, reflecting a balanced tug-of-war between buyers and sellers. Price action remains tightly compressed between 74.3 and 75, suggesting low volatility and indecision. Despite the lack of directional momentum, the structure still leans slightly constructive as higher lows are attempting to form near the short-term average, indicating that bullish participants are quietly defending downside pressure.

MACD positive 0.664 suggests mild bullish momentum, but absence of signal line prevents confirmation of crossover strength. Price holding above the 20-period SMA at 74.28 reinforces a short-term bullish bias, though the narrow spread indicates weak conviction. With RSI unavailable, momentum clarity is limited, so traders should rely more heavily on price structure and moving average interaction to assess continuation or potential reversal risk in the near term.

Key support is established around 74.3, aligning closely with the recent low and the 20-period SMA, forming a confluence zone that buyers are currently defending. Immediate resistance is seen at 75, a psychological barrier where price has repeatedly stalled during recent sessions. A decisive break above this level could open the path toward 75.5–76, while failure to hold above support may expose a retest of lower consolidation levels near 74.

Given the current range-bound structure, a mean-reversion strategy remains appropriate for short-term traders, buying near support and taking profits into resistance. Breakout traders should monitor a sustained close above 75 for confirmation of upward expansion toward higher liquidity zones. Risk management is essential, with stop-loss levels ideally placed below 74 to avoid false breakdowns. Until volatility expands, disciplined position sizing and patience are key to navigating choppy conditions.
